__fixture__.name: repair_card_deny_all_blocked_extension_egress
__fixture__.scenario: Deny-all posture consent_blocked_by_policy card. The transport posture carries offline_or_deny_all_state = deny_all_enforced; an installed extension's network capability would mint an outbound_denied_by_deny_all_profile network attribution if invoked. The card explains why the extension's network capability is unavailable from the current posture, names the org_admin authority required to change the deny-all posture, and links the network attribution and policy bundle that explain the deny. Because consent is consent_blocked_by_policy, the card MUST link at least one evidence record (the schema enforces this), and the only side effect declared is no_side_effect_inspect_only since the action cannot land from this posture. Demonstrates that deny-all posture remains explicit rather than being inferred from a generic 'extension blocked' message.
contract_sections: Offline, mirror-only, and deny-all examples remain explicit

title_label_text: Extension network capability blocked under deny-all profile
what_changes_sentence: Explains why this extension's network capability is unavailable from the current deny-all posture and names the org admin who can change the deny-all rule; nothing changes on this device or in the extension itself.
where_it_applies_sentence: Observable only in the org admin console; no local setting, workspace pin, remote target, or managed provider is touched until an admin changes the deny-all rule from the admin console.

evidence_links.evidence_note: Attribution showing an outbound_denied_by_deny_all_profile outcome under deny_all_enforced posture for the extension's network capability.
Active bundle whose deny-all rule is in force; the rule's opaque policy_rule_ref is named on the attribution's deny_reason_detail.
Posture object naming deny_all_enforced; collapses any extension-host egress above local_only to outbound_denied_by_deny_all_profile.
